faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Lynbrook home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moths include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cashmere garments, silken tubes or cases in closet corners, adult moths flying near wardrobes (especially at dusk), and webbing or cocoons on clothing or carpet edges. You may also notice larvae that look like small cream-colored worms on fabric surfaces.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in Lynbrook?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control services in Lynbrook typically range from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of infestation, size of affected areas, and number of treatments required. A single-room treatment starts around $550, while whole-home infestations requiring multiple visits and extensive treatment can reach $5,000. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment eliminates adult moths and larvae within 24-48 hours, but complete eradication typically requires 2-3 treatments spaced several weeks apart to address eggs and newly hatched larvae. Most Lynbrook homes are moth-free within 6-8 weeks with our comprehensive treatment plan, which includes follow-up inspections to ensure complete elimination.

faqs.question: Can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes! We recommend regular vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing seasonal clothing in sealed containers, dry cleaning or freezing woolens before storage, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and maintaining low humidity levels. Our technicians provide detailed prevention strategies specific to your Lynbrook home during service.
